judgments.reasoning: No candidate addresses try-catch around dynamic import await; C6 concerns eager evaluation vs lazy loading, a different issue.
C1 identifies the same forEach(async) fire-and-forget pattern causing unhandled promise rejections and silent failure.
No candidate addresses the TwoFactor/BackupCode.tsx naming inconsistency.
No candidate addresses the wrong error message text referencing login in a disable endpoint.
C1 identifies the same case-sensitive indexOf backup code comparison with no toLowerCase normalization.
C3 identifies the same TOCTOU race where concurrent requests can both consume the same single-use backup code.
C1 identifies the same unguarded .integration access on potentially undefined mainHostDestinationCalendar after empty array destructure.
No candidate addresses the redundant optional chaining on mainHostDestinationCalendar?.integration after an explicit ternary guard.
C2 identifies the same tautological find where the else-branch predicate compares cal.externalId against the already-falsy externalCalendarId.
No candidate addresses the IS_TEAM_BILLING_ENABLED slug logic inversion.
No candidate addresses the Calendar interface contract break from the new credentialId parameter in createEvent.
No candidates present.
No candidates present.
C2 identifies the same forEach fire-and-forget for deleteScheduledEmailReminder/SMS with silently swallowed async errors.
C1 identifies the same issue: immediateDelete=true cancels SendGrid but returns without deleting the WorkflowReminder DB record.
C1 identifies the same end boundary bug where slotStartTime is used instead of slotEndTime.
C2 identifies the same === reference comparison on Dayjs objects that always returns false.
C1 identifies the same bug where parseRefreshTokenResponse assigns the literal string "refresh_token" when the token is missing.
No candidate addresses invalid Zod schema syntax with computed property keys.
C2 identifies the same issue: parseRefreshTokenResponse returns a SafeParseReturnType wrapper that gets persisted instead of the credential payload.
C3 identifies the same issue: sync-mode returns a raw fetch Response causing callers to crash accessing .data.error.
G5 is the same mechanism as G4 (C3 already matched to G4); no remaining candidate covers only the res.data undefined access path.
No candidate specifically addresses the non-atomic stale-read race for retryCount increment; C2 covers a different catch-block re-increment scenario.
C1 identifies the same OR-branch deleteMany bug that incorrectly deletes non-SMS reminders when retryCount > 1.
C2 identifies the same case-sensitive blacklist comparison allowing email bypass with uppercase input.
C1 identifies the same AND vs OR logic error in the team admin/owner permission check.
C3 identifies the same bug where sendAddGuestsEmails is called with the original unfiltered guests instead of uniqueGuests.
No candidate addresses the missing deduplication within the input list before createMany.
No candidate addresses the empty string initial value issue in MultiEmail.
No candidate addresses the empty data object preventing @updatedAt from being refreshed in updateManyByCredentialId.
C1 identifies the same macOS-specific sed -i syntax that fails on GNU/Linux.
C1 identifies the same duplicate self.downsize definition where the 3-arg form silently replaces the 5-arg form.
C2 identifies the same hardcoded 10 MB limit that bypasses the dynamic SiteSettings max_size_kb setting.
No candidate addresses the animated GIF failure when passing 80% as dimensions to gifsicle which expects WxH format.
C1 identifies the same nil dereference crash when no TopicUser record exists for the current user.
No candidate addresses the stopNotificiationsText typo.
No candidate addresses the side-effecting read in BlockedEmail.should_block_email? that updates statistics during a check operation.
C2 identifies the same missing trailing anchor allowing subdomain-based whitelist bypass via the email restriction regex.
C6 identifies the same SSRF vulnerability from using open-uri with unvalidated URLs in poll_feed and import_remote.
C1 identifies the same substring-match origin validation flaw that allows spoofed-domain bypass.
No candidate addresses the postMessage targetOrigin using full referrer URL instead of scheme+host+port.
No candidate addresses the X-Frame-Options: ALLOWALL setting that disables clickjacking protection.
No candidate specifically addresses NoMethodError on nil contents or the XSS from unescaped url in TopicEmbed.import context.
C2 identifies the same invalid ERB "end if" syntax that raises SyntaxError on every render.
No candidate addresses the float/flexbox mixing issue or the removed float-based right alignment for .d-header .panel.
C1 identifies the same fabricated -ms-align-items property that never existed in any IE/Edge version.
No candidate addresses the missing ? suffix on include_website_name that breaks Rails serializer conditional inclusion.
C1 identifies the same lightness inversion (30%->70%) in topic-post stylesheet link color, matching G1 direction and theme.
C4 identifies the same 30%->50% lightness change for .name color in desktop/user.scss card-content.
C2 identifies the same direction inversion (70%->30%, making text darker) in a stylesheet, consistent with G3 description.
C3 identifies the same unhandled async findMembers() call in setupController that creates a race condition.
C4 identifies the same offset clamping to user_count that produces an empty final page.
No candidate addresses the HTTP method mismatch where the test uses PUT but remove_member expects DELETE.
No candidates present.
No candidates present.
C4 identifies the same NoMethodError in EmbeddableHost before_validation when the host attribute is nil.
C2 identifies the same nil dereference in the destroy action when the EmbeddableHost record is not found.
No candidate addresses the missing case normalization for the host parameter in record_for_host.
No candidate addresses the migration bypassing model validation via raw SQL INSERT.
No candidate addresses the race condition where concurrent requests bypass the device count check.
No candidate addresses the breaking change where anonymous auth now fails entirely on ErrDeviceLimitReached.
No candidate addresses the compile error from dbSession.Exec called with a []interface{} splice.
C2 identifies the same misleading ErrDeviceLimitReached return when no rows were updated due to an expired existing device.
No candidate addresses the time window calculation inconsistency using UpdatedAt.UTC().Add vs device.UpdatedAt.
No candidate addresses the asymmetric logic where cached denials are ignored while cached grants are returned immediately.
C2 identifies the same test fixture bug where permCache is set to false instead of true, contradicting the test comment.
No candidate addresses the nil request panic in ContextualLoggerMiddleware methods.
C2 identifies the same removal of traceID from plugin request log lines during the middleware refactoring.
No candidates present.
C1 identifies the same missing key prop for GrafanaRuleListItem in the FilterView list mapping.
No candidate addresses the RuleActionsButtons missing the Grafana Ruler rule needed by SilenceGrafanaRuleDrawer.
No candidate addresses the context created with d.Log instead of the enriched log variable.
C1 identifies the same recordLegacyDuration vs recordStorageDuration confusion on the storage failure path.
C4 identifies the same issue of passing name instead of options.Kind as the kind metric label.
C1 identifies the same misuse of Error log level for normal cleanup success path information.
C1 identifies the same incomplete double-checked locking where the cache is not re-checked after acquiring the write lock.
No candidate addresses the unconditional assignment of the fetch result to cache regardless of error status.
C1 identifies the same logic bug where enableSqlExpressions always returns false regardless of feature flag state.
C2 identifies the same not-implemented stub in sql.DB.RunCommands (G2 covers multiple stubs; C2 is the strongest match).
C1 identifies the same race condition in BuildIndex where concurrent goroutines can build the same index simultaneously.
No candidate addresses the TotalDocs() race with concurrent index creation.
C1 identifies the same arity mismatch where UsernameForm calls isConditionalPasskeysEnabled() with no arguments but the method requires a UserModel parameter.
No candidate addresses the conditional passkey UI not being set up when context.getUser() is null on the initial login page.
No candidate addresses the recursive caching call that uses session instead of the delegate provider.
C1 identifies the same test cleanup bug where literal "alias" is used instead of the dynamically generated IDP alias strings.
C3 identifies the same bug where getBouncyCastleProvider() returns the default JRE keystore provider instead of the BouncyCastle provider.
C1 identifies the same dead code where ASN1Encoder instances are created and written to but their results are silently discarded.
No candidate addresses the picocli.exit() method calling System.exit() directly, which is problematic for testing and embedders.
No candidate addresses the AdminPermissions event listener incorrectly guarded by the V1 feature flag instead of V2.
No candidate addresses the hasPermission resource lookup using wrong owner, causing it to never find per-client resources.
No candidate addresses getClientsWithPermission returning type-level resource name instead of actual client IDs.
No candidate specifically addresses the incorrect permission check in canManage(); C1 covers canView filter removal which is a different authorization issue.
C2 identifies the same bug where getGroupIdsWithViewPermission passes a resource UUID to findByName causing all per-group permission lookups to miss.
No candidate addresses Italian text appearing in the Lithuanian (messages_lt.properties) translation file.
No candidate addresses Traditional Chinese terms appearing in the Simplified Chinese (zh_CN) translation file.
No candidate addresses the anchor sanitization logic consuming English matcher groups without validation.
No candidate addresses the santizeAnchors typo (missing "i").
C1 identifies the same bug where the constructor checks grantType for null twice instead of validating rawTokenId.
C2 identifies the same inverted return logic and wrong grant-shortcut substring range in isAccessTokenId.
No candidate addresses the Javadoc discrepancy between "3-letter shortcut" and actual 2-letter shortcut usage.
No candidate addresses the test catching generic RuntimeException instead of the specific IllegalArgumentException.

C2 identifies the same null return from getSubGroupsCount() causing NPE via auto-unboxing at callers.
C1 identifies the same reader thread not being joined before assertions, creating a race condition in the test.
C1 identifies the same Django QuerySet negative slicing crash in OptimizedCursorPaginator when cursor.offset < 0.
C3 identifies the same AttributeError on None.has_global_access when organization_context.member is None for API key/org token requests.
C2 identifies the same TypeError from applying math.floor/ceil to a datetime attribute in get_item_key.
C1 identifies the same negative-offset QuerySet slice crash in OptimizedCursorPaginator.
C2 identifies the same BasePaginator negative-offset pass-through that crashes the Django ORM.
No candidate addresses the get_item_key TypeError from floor/ceil applied to a datetime key in this PR context; C2 of PR41 was for a different PR.
No candidate addresses the sample_rate = 0.0 falsy check being skipped when upsampling should apply.
No candidate addresses the non-deterministic hash() usage for cache keys across processes.
No candidate addresses the upsampling eligibility check passing the outer dataset instead of scoped_dataset.
No candidate addresses the null reference when github_authenticated_user state is missing.
No candidate addresses the OAuth state using the static pipeline.signature instead of a per-request random value.
No candidate addresses the KeyError from accessing integration.metadata[sender] without checking key existence.
No candidate addresses breaking changes in error response format.
No candidate addresses the detector validator using the wrong key when updating type.
No candidate addresses the zip(error_ids, events.values()) ordering assumption with non-deterministic dict values.
No candidate addresses the inconsistent metric tagging using both "shard" and "shards".
No candidate addresses the fixed sleep in tests being flaky.
No candidate addresses the isinstance check always being false for SpawnProcess.
No candidate addresses the monkeypatched time.sleep making the test_consumer.py sleep ineffective.
C2 identifies the same issue of the loop exhausting timeout on next_step.join, leaving no time to terminate remaining flusher processes.
C1 identifies the same shared mutable default where timezone.now() is evaluated at class definition time, producing identical timestamps.
No candidate addresses the test_from_dict_inalid_data typo.
No candidate addresses the method name saying empty_array when it tests an empty dict.
No candidate addresses to_dict() returning a datetime object that may fail JSON serialization.

No candidate addresses the unnecessary DB query fetching MonitorCheckIn objects when data is already available in previous_checkins.
No candidate addresses queue.shutdown(immediate=False) potentially not existing in the standard Python queue module.
No candidate addresses the magic number 50 used repeatedly for max_wait.
No candidate addresses the docstring mismatch in test_thread_queue_parallel_error_handling.
C1 identifies the same MetricAlertDetectorHandler abstract method implementation gap causing TypeError on instantiation.
